question_text:
Partition to K Equal Sum Subsets

Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return true if it is possible to divide this array into k non-empty subsets whose sums are all equal.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= k <= nums.length <= 16
  • 1 <= nums[i] <= 104
  • The frequency of each element is in the range [1, 4].
input_format: An integer array nums and integer k.
output_format: true if it is possible to partition nums into k equal-sum subsets, false otherwise.
explanation:
Backtracking with Bucket Pruning

Sort nums descending. Use k buckets each targeting sum/k. At each step, try placing nums[idx] into a bucket where it fits. Skip buckets with the same current sum (pruning identical states). Return true when all elements are placed.

Time: O(k × 2^N). Space: O(N).
answer:
def canPartitionKSubsets(nums, k):
    total = sum(nums)
    if total % k != 0: return False
    target = total // k
    nums.sort(reverse=True)
    if nums[0] > target: return False
    buckets = [0] * k
    def dfs(idx):
        if idx == len(nums): return True
        seen = set()
        for i in range(k):
            if buckets[i] in seen: continue
            if buckets[i] + nums[idx] <= target:
                seen.add(buckets[i])
                buckets[i] += nums[idx]
                if dfs(idx + 1): return True
                buckets[i] -= nums[idx]
        return False
    return dfs(0)
